headline: The Week in Numbers: SpaceX's IPO plans, Samsung settles dispute
summary: STORY: From SpaceX's blockbuster IPO to Samsung's shares surging after a pay dispute... This is the Week in Numbers.:: The Week in Numbers:: $1 trillionOver $1 trillion is the value Elon Musk's SpaceX could reach in its stock market listing.That would immediately make it one of the world’s most valuable publicly traded firms.Musk has grown SpaceX into the world's largest rocket business by launching thousands of Starlink internet satellites.And pioneering reusable rockets.SpaceX is aiming to make its market debut as early as mid-June, with a roadshow for investors due to start around a week earlier.:: $67 billion$67 billion is the value of a deal for NextEra Energy to buy Dominion Energy in an all-stock transaction. The two powerhouse utility companies are merging as the AI boom fuels data center demand. The deal is one of the biggest in the U.S. power industry, and will create the world's largest regulated electric utility company by market value. :: $91 billion$91 billion is how much Nvidia expects to make in second quarter sales. The world's most valuable company forecast revenue for the quarter above Wall Street expectations.And assured investors that new customers would let it maintain blockbuster rates of growth.   For the first quarter, Nvidia reported revenue of $81.6 billion, also beating forecasts.:: 34% 34% was how much registrations of new EVs in Europe grew by year-on-year in AprilThis shift was triggered by an upsurge in international oil prices to well above $100 a barrel.  During past spikes in fuel prices, consumers also switched to more fuel-efficient cars - but then switched back.  But, as several European car brands reported a hike in EV orders since the war began, Europe’s senior auto correspondent Nick Carey said this time could be different"In markets where EV penetration has already reached a high level, and where charging infrastructure is already quite mature, this is seen as a seismic shift where people are unlikely to go back.":: 8%8% was how much Samsung Electronic shares surged by after it clinched a deal with its South Korean union...to avert an 18-day strike by some 48,000 workers.The agreement sparked relief, as a strike could inflict significant damage on the economy and dent global chip supply.In terms of the deal, one union source says a memory chip worker with a base salary of about $53,000, is expected to receive a bonus of around $416,000.

raw.headline: The Week in Numbers: SpaceX's IPO plans, Samsung settles dispute
raw.summary: STORY: From SpaceX's blockbuster IPO to Samsung's shares surging after a pay dispute... This is the Week in Numbers.:: The Week in Numbers:: $1 trillionOver $1 trillion is the value Elon Musk's SpaceX could reach in its stock market listing.That would immediately make it one of the world’s most valuable publicly traded firms.Musk has grown SpaceX into the world's largest rocket business by launching thousands of Starlink internet satellites.And pioneering reusable rockets.SpaceX is aiming to make its market debut as early as mid-June, with a roadshow for investors due to start around a week earlier.:: $67 billion$67 billion is the value of a deal for NextEra Energy to buy Dominion Energy in an all-stock transaction. The two powerhouse utility companies are merging as the AI boom fuels data center demand. The deal is one of the biggest in the U.S. power industry, and will create the world's largest regulated electric utility company by market value. :: $91 billion$91 billion is how much Nvidia expects to make in second quarter sales. The world's most valuable company forecast revenue for the quarter above Wall Street expectations.And assured investors that new customers would let it maintain blockbuster rates of growth.   For the first quarter, Nvidia reported revenue of $81.6 billion, also beating forecasts.:: 34% 34% was how much registrations of new EVs in Europe grew by year-on-year in AprilThis shift was triggered by an upsurge in international oil prices to well above $100 a barrel.  During past spikes in fuel prices, consumers also switched to more fuel-efficient cars - but then switched back.  But, as several European car brands reported a hike in EV orders since the war began, Europe’s senior auto correspondent Nick Carey said this time could be different"In markets where EV penetration has already reached a high level, and where charging infrastructure is already quite mature, this is seen as a seismic shift where people are unlikely to go back.":: 8%8% was how much Samsung Electronic shares surged by after it clinched a deal with its South Korean union...to avert an 18-day strike by some 48,000 workers.The agreement sparked relief, as a strike could inflict significant damage on the economy and dent global chip supply.In terms of the deal, one union source says a memory chip worker with a base salary of about $53,000, is expected to receive a bonus of around $416,000.

headline: 14 Press Releases You Need to See This Week
summary: With thousands of press releases published each week, it can be difficult to keep up with everything on PR Newswire. To help journalists and consumers stay on top of the week's most newsworthy and popular releases, here's a recap of some major stories from the week that shouldn't be missed.

headline: Assessing Dominion Energy (D) Valuation After Recent Share Price Momentum
summary: Recent performance snapshot Dominion Energy (D) has drawn attention after a stretch of gains, with the stock up 0.8% in the last session, 8.4% over the past week, and about 12% over the past month. See our latest analysis for Dominion Energy. This recent upswing comes on top of a 15.3% year to date share price return and a 28.4% one year total shareholder return, suggesting momentum has picked up after a quieter stretch. If Dominion Energy’s move has you thinking about the broader shift...

headline: NextEra Energy Broadens Energy Mix With US$1.3b Caliber Acquisition
summary: NextEra Energy (NYSE:NEE) agreed to acquire Caliber Resource Partners for about US$1.3b. The company also plans to form a joint venture with Quantum Capital Group tied to these assets. The transactions expand NextEra's presence in U.S. oil and gas alongside its existing operations. For a company widely associated with renewables and regulated utilities, NYSE:NEE stepping further into oil and gas marks a clear broadening of its energy mix. The move comes shortly after NextEra announced a...
